product_overview: TestProof analyzes GitHub repositories, pull requests, and issues to surface test-readiness, contributor signals, and recommended TestSprite workflows. Users paste a GitHub URL, the app calls internal analysis endpoints, and structured report cards are rendered with recommendations and preflight checklists.
core_goals: Provide fast, actionable analysis for GitHub repositories, pull requests, and issues to prioritize testing and contributor work.
Detect URL type (repo / PR / issue), validate inputs, and surface clear error states for invalid or missing data.
Render structured report cards with readiness signals, recommended TestSprite workflows, and preflight checklists.
Keep UI responsive with mode-specific placeholders, loading skeletons, and clear mode switching behavior.

features.name: Repo Analysis
features.description: Accept a GitHub repository URL and evaluate test-readiness, repo health, contributor readiness, and generate a recommended TestSprite workflow and pre-flight checklist.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Paste a GitHub repository URL in the input field -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze returns 200 -> See ReportCard with sections: Repo Summary, Repo Health, Contributor Readiness, TestSprite Readiness, Recommended Workflow, Pre-flight Checklist, Final Verdict
Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Paste an invalid string into the input field -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See "Invalid GitHub URL" error message

Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Paste a well-formed repo URL that does not exist -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze returns 404 -> See "Repo not found" error message

Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Paste a valid repo URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze returns 401/403 due to missing GITHUB_TOKEN -> See "GitHub authentication required" or permission error message

features.name: PR Analysis
features.description: Accept a GitHub pull request URL to classify PR focus, show changed areas, and produce a TestSprite workflow recommendation with a preflight checklist.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Paste a GitHub PR URL in the input field -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze-pr returns 200 -> See PRReportCard with PR metadata, focus type, changed areas, Recommended Workflow, and Pre-flight Checklist
Navigate to / -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Paste a repo URL instead of a PR URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See format error indicating a PR URL is required

Navigate to / -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Paste a well-formed PR URL for a missing PR -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze-pr returns 404 -> See "Pull request not found" error message

Navigate to / -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Paste a valid PR URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze-pr returns 403 due to GitHub rate limiting -> See "Rate limit exceeded" or GitHub API error message

features.name: Issue Analysis
features.description: Accept a GitHub issue URL and assess work status, quality signals, whether someone is working on it, and provide contributor recommendations.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Paste a GitHub issue URL in the input field -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze-issue returns 200 -> See IssueReportCard with sections: Issue Summary, Work Status, Issue Quality, Final Recommendation

Navigate to / -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Paste a PR URL instead of an issue URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See format error indicating the URL points to a pull request

Navigate to / -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Paste a well-formed issue URL that does not exist -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST /api/analyze-issue returns 404 -> See "Issue not found" error message

Navigate to / -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Paste a valid issue URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> Network failure occurs -> See "Failed to connect to the server" error message

features.name: Mode Toggle
features.description: Switch between Repo, Pull Request, and Issue analysis modes; switching updates UI affordances and clears previous results.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Observe input placeholder and button color for repo mode -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Observe input placeholder and button color change for PR mode -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Observe input placeholder and button color change for issue mode

Navigate to / -> Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode -> Paste a PR URL and click "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> While results are visible, click "Repo" tab to switch modes -> Observe previous results cleared on mode switch -> See input placeholder and button color update for repo mode

Navigate to / -> Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode -> Paste an issue URL -> Click "Analyze" button to submit -> Immediately click another mode tab -> Observe previous results cleared and no report shown for the previous mode

features.name: Input Validation and Error Handling
features.description: Validate URLs server-side across all endpoints and present clear UI error messages for invalid formats, wrong mode inputs, 404s, auth failures, rate limits, and network errors.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click the appropriate mode tab for the URL type -> Paste a valid GitHub URL for that mode -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See loading skeleton while analysis runs -> POST to the corresponding endpoint (/api/analyze, /api/analyze-pr, or /api/analyze-issue) returns 200 -> See structured report card
Navigate to / -> Click any mode tab -> Paste a non-GitHub URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See "Invalid GitHub URL" error message

Navigate to / -> Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode -> Paste a PR URL into the repo input -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> See a format error indicating a repo URL is required for repo mode

Navigate to / -> Click the appropriate mode tab -> Paste a valid GitHub URL that points to a missing resource -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> POST to the corresponding endpoint returns 404 -> See "not found" error message relevant to the resource type

Navigate to / -> Click the appropriate mode tab -> Paste a valid GitHub URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> POST to the endpoint returns 401/403 due to missing or invalid GITHUB_TOKEN -> See "GitHub authentication required" or permission error message

Navigate to / -> Click the appropriate mode tab -> Paste a valid GitHub URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> POST to the endpoint returns 403 due to rate limiting -> See "Rate limit exceeded" error message

Navigate to / -> Click the appropriate mode tab -> Paste a valid GitHub URL -> Click the "Analyze" button to submit -> Network failure occurs before receiving a response -> See "Failed to connect to the server" error message

routes.description: Main single-page app with mode toggle (Repo / PR / Issue) and analysis results
POST endpoint that analyzes a GitHub repository URL for test-readiness and contributor signals
POST endpoint that analyzes a GitHub pull request URL, classifying focus type and generating TestSprite workflow
POST endpoint that analyzes a GitHub issue URL for work status, quality, and recommendation

features.name: Repo Analysis
features.description: User enters a GitHub repository URL to evaluate test-readiness, repo health, contributor readiness, and get a TestSprite workflow suggestion
user_interactions: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Paste a GitHub repository URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo) in the input field
Click the "Analyze" button to submit
See loading skeleton while analysis runs

user_interactions.View ReportCard with 7 sections: Repo Summary, Repo Health, Contributor Readiness, TestSprite Readiness, Recommended Workflow, Pre-flight Checklist, Final Verdict

user_interactions: See error message if URL is invalid or repo not found

features.name: PR Analysis
features.description: User enters a GitHub pull request URL to get PR focus classification, TestSprite workflow recommendation, and preflight checklist
user_interactions: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode
Paste a GitHub PR URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123) in the input field
Click the "Analyze" button to submit
See loading skeleton while analysis runs
View PRReportCard with PR metadata, focus type, changed areas, workflow section, and preflight checklist
See error message if URL is invalid, PR not found, or a repo URL is accidentally entered

features.name: Issue Analysis
features.description: User enters a GitHub issue URL to assess work status, quality signals, and get a contributor recommendation
user_interactions: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode
Paste a GitHub issue URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/456) in the input field
Click the "Analyze" button to submit
See loading skeleton while analysis runs

user_interactions.View IssueReportCard with 4 sections: Issue Summary, Work Status, Issue Quality, Final Recommendation

user_interactions: See error message if URL is invalid, issue not found, or a PR URL is accidentally entered in issue mode

features.name: Mode Toggle
features.description: User can switch between Repo, Pull Request, and Issue analysis modes; switching clears previous results
user_interactions: Click Repo tab (emerald) to enter repo mode
Click Pull Request tab (violet) to enter PR mode
Click Issue tab (sky blue) to enter issue mode
Observe input placeholder and button color change per mode
Observe previous results cleared on mode switch

features.name: Input Validation and Error Handling
features.description: All three API routes validate URLs server-side; the UI shows error messages for invalid URLs, wrong mode, 404s, and network failures
user_interactions: Enter a non-GitHub URL and see "Invalid GitHub URL" error
Enter a PR URL in repo mode and see format error
Enter an issue URL in PR mode and see format error
Enter a PR URL in issue mode and see "That URL points to a pull request" error
Enter a URL for a non-existent repo/PR/issue and see 404 error message
See "Failed to connect to the server" on network failure

known_limitations.issue: GitHub API requests require a valid GITHUB_TOKEN env var; without it all fetches return 401/403
known_limitations.impact: All analysis endpoints will fail if GITHUB_TOKEN is missing or expired

known_limitations.issue: GitHub API rate limiting (60 req/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr authenticated) can cause analysis to fail under load
known_limitations.impact: Concurrent tests hitting real GitHub API may get 403 rate-limit responses

known_limitations.issue: Issue timeline API uses mockingbird-preview header which is a GitHub preview feature
known_limitations.impact: Timeline-based work status signals may be unreliable if preview API changes
